raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint due to American Expresss refusal to provide appropriate protection in a dispute involving a clearly fraudulent merchant. Despite presenting extensive evidence that I never received the items I paid for, and that the merchant used falsified tracking information, American Express has denied my claim and is still holding me responsible for the charge. 

The dispute concerns a transaction with XXXX, a deceptive online retailer that submitted a fraudulent tracking document to American Express. The tracking shows a supposed delivery through a shipping company called XXXX name that is not affiliated with any legitimate XXXX shipping XXXX. The merchant used XXXX, a public tracking aggregator that does not verify the legitimacy of the shipping provider or the delivery. 

The tracking number provided by the merchant : Does not include my full address or name. 
Lists only a general location ( XXXX XXXX, XXXX  ) with no confirmation of delivery, no zip code, no signature, and no proof that any package was ever delivered to my home. 
Can not be verified on any official XXXX XXXX website ( XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, etc. ). 

I have gone above and beyond to prove this is a fraudulent transaction : I provided evidence showing the merchants address and phone number are fake. 
I submitted screenshots of multiple outreach attempts to the merchant, all of which went ignored. 
I documented consumer warnings about this website from the BXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, and other consumer protection sites. 
I began reviewing security footage from my home on the date of the claimed delivery to confirm no package was delivered. 

Despite this overwhelming documentation, American Express has taken the merchants false tracking information at face valuewithout validating the shipping provider or requiring proof of actual delivery to my address. As a result, I am being forced to pay for goods I never received, from a merchant that is clearly operating under deceptive and possibly criminal intent. 

I am requesting that the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau investigate American Expresss failure to properly investigate this dispute, and hold them accountable for their lack of due diligence in allowing fraudulent documentation to outweigh legitimate consumer protection. 

Thank you for your time and assistance in this matter.
